Case Study 1 · Excel

Excel KPI Command Center

A formula-driven workbook that lets a business leader monitor performance, spot problems, and decide quickly. Six tabs flow from raw data to a one-screen executive dashboard.

Formula-driven reportingReusable KPI structureExecutive-ready dashboardError checks & validation
Gross margin = (Revenue - Cost) / Revenue
Conversion rate = Conversions / Visitors
AOV = Revenue / Orders
WoW change = Rev[t] / Rev[t-1] - 1
Variance = Actual / Target - 1
Download Excel_KPI_Command_Center.xlsx
Case Study 2 · Slides

Executive Insight Deck

An eight-slide deck that frames a business question, walks through three insights, and lands a clear recommendation. One idea per slide, minimal text, decision-ready.

Executive communicationSlide design disciplineData storytellingDecision-ready presentation

Case Study 3 · Evaluation

Spreadsheet Evaluation Pack

A rubric-based comparison of two AI-generated dashboards. Explicit 1–5 criteria, a formula-driven scorecard, and a written rationale for the winner.

Rubric-based evaluationAI-training mindsetStructured comparisonWritten rationale

Case Study 4 · Real Data

Commodity Shock & Inflation

A real-data study built from free public sources (FRED & the World Bank): the 2022 energy and food price shock and the inflation that followed. Framed as timeline and co-movement, with an explicit caveat that it is not a sole-cause claim.

Real public data (FRED, World Bank)Reproducible vendored snapshotIndexed time-seriesCareful, caveated framing
Index = price[t] / price[Jan 2022] × 100
Inflation jump = CPI%[2022] - CPI%[2021]
Source: FRED (monthly) + World Bank (annual), vendored snapshot
Download Commodity_Shock_and_Inflation.xlsx

Simon Sangla · simonsangla@gmail.com

Built reproducibly with Python (openpyxl, python-pptx). Screenshots rendered from the actual files via headless LibreOffice. Case studies 1-3 use synthetic, deterministic sample data (seed 42) from one shared source so the numbers agree everywhere; case study 4 uses real public data (FRED and the World Bank), vendored as a reproducible snapshot.
